sourceMovie.title: Lockout
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Lockout for the same reason it broke through in 2012: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Stephen St. Leger keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Looper
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lockout clicked for you, Looper (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lockout?
faq.answer: Try Looper, The Island, Oblivion—each shares DNA with Lockout while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lockout?
faq.answer: Lockout (2012) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lockout worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
